data.title: ServiceTitan soars nearly 40% after raising nearly $625M in IPO
data.description:
• Shares of ServiceTitan (NASDAQ:TTAN) soared 42% on Thursday and opened at $101 per share as the newly public cloud service software company raised $624.8M in its initial public offering.
  • Glendale, Calif.-based ServiceTitan sold 8.8M shares at a price of $71 each in its offering.
  • The company, which provides cloud software services to home and commercial contractors in the trades' industry, granted its underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 1.32M shares.
  • ServiceTitan said its revenue grew approximately 24.2% to $363.32M for the six months ended July 31, 2024, compared to the six months ended July 31, 2023, according to filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • For the fiscal year 2024 ended Jan. 31, revenue soared about 31.3% to $614.34M. As of July 31, 2024, ServiceTitan had cash and cash equivalents of $128.1M.

data.title: Pfizer's Ibrance shows promise in HR+, HER2+ breast cancer
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) said a Phase 3 study of its drug Ibrance in combination with stand-of-care therapies was found to help extend progression-free survival, or PFS, in patients with HR+, HER2+ metastatic breast cancer.
  • The study, called PATINA, showed that median PFS was 44.3 months for patients treated with Ibrance in combination with anti-HER2 therapy and endocrine therapy versus 29.1 months for patients treated with anti-HER2 therapy and endocrine therapy alone.
  • Overall survival, the study’s secondary endpoint, was not yet mature at the time of the analysis, according to a statement.
  • The results were presented at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium.
  • Ibrance is already approved for the treatment of advanced or metastatic HR+, HER2- breast cancer in combination with an aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant.

data.title: Patterson Cos. trades near deal price even as analysts see higher bid unlikely
data.description:
Patterson Cos. (NASDAQ:PDCO) earlier traded through its $31.35 a share sale to Patient Square even as analysts downplayed the idea of a higher offer for the dental products company.

Some investors may believe that Patterson (PDCO) could get a higher bid in a 40-day go shop period after it agreed to the $4 billion sale to Patient Square.

"There is certainly potential for another bid to come, but given the recent trading valuation and general questions on near term dental market demand, we see the multiple paid for the transaction as reasonable and thus view any meaningful "topticking" bid as unlikely," Leerink analyst Michael Cherny, who has a market perform rating on PDCO, wrote on Wednesday.

While it's possible Patterson (PDCO) may see other offers, a strategic buyer is less likely, Morgan Stanley analyst Erin Wright wrote in a note on Wednesday.

"Overall, we view the relative concentration of each segment as inherently limiting the potential optionality with few viable strategic combinations, leaving likely only financial suitors, in our view," Wright, who has an equal-weight rating on PDCO wrote.

A financial buyer typically can't pay as much as a strategic suitor, who can pay more due to the synergies in a transaction.

"While it is possible that PDCO could be an interesting asset for a strategic buyer (was consensus investor opinion post last week), we see that potential as somewhat muted given a combination of either anti-trust concerns or other strategic plans and see Patient Square as likely to prevail (take out valuation of ~11.5x CY25 EBITDA is attractive)," Evercore ISI analyst Elizabeth Anderson wrote in a note on Thursday.

More on Patterson

  • Patterson Companies, Inc. (PDCO) Q2 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Patterson Could Struggle Under Potential Trump Tariffs, But It Looks A Buy Right Now
  • Patterson jumps 34% on $4.1B Patient Square's take private deal (update)
  • Patterson reports mixed Q2 results; lowers full year outlook

data.title: Most and least shorted S&P 500 financial stocks in November
data.description:
Average short interest across S&P 500 financial stocks rose to 1.58% of shares float as of the end of November, compared to 1.50% at October end.

The S&P 500's financial sector (NYSEARCA:XLF) was up 32.5%, as compared to the broader S&P 500 index's rise of 27.6% since the start of the year.

The largest contributor to the index, Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) had a short interest of 1.1% of the total float, while the next biggest contributor, J.P. Morgan's (JPM) short interest, stood at 1.1%.

Industry Analysis:

Consumer Finance continued to dominate at the top and continued being the most shorted industry within the financial sector, with 2.60% short interest as of November end, up from 2.19% as of October end.

Capital Markets is the second most shorted industry within the financial sector, with 2.03% short interest as of end-November, down from 2.05% as of end-October.

Financial Services, came in at third, with short interest at 1.93% at November end, up from 1.83% as of October end.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float)

Most shorted stocks:

Franklin Resources (BEN) – 9.34% vs 8.89% last month

FactSet Research System (FDS) – 6.16% vs 5.92%

T Rowe Price (TROW) – 4.83% vs 4.96%

Capital One Financial (COF) 4.32% vs 2.97%

Regions Financial (RF) – 3.77% vs 4.50%

Least shorted stocks

Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC) - 0.71% vs 0.88% last month

Chubb (CB) - 0.71% vs 0.86%

Progressive (PGR) – 0.74% vs 0.87%

Mastercard (MA) – 0.77% vs 0.78%

Loews (L) – 0.78% vs 0.86%

data.title: Western Digital drops as company presents at Barclays conference
data.description:
• Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) shares dropped sharply on Thursday, turning lower by more than 5%, as the company spoke at a Barclays investor conference.
  • Shares of Seagate Technology (STX), which competes with Western Digital, and Micron (MU), also turned lower.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Some ECB policymakers backed a 50-basis-point cut - report
data.description:
A handful of European Central Bank policymakers initially argued that lower inflation and growth forecasts, combined with potential U.S. tariffs, warranted a 50-basis-point rate cut, rather than the 25-bp reduction the central bank's Governing Council eventually agreed upon, according to a media report.

With the 25-bp cut, the ECB's Governing Council kept the option open for further easing as the euro-area economy still faces political instability at home, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing three unnamed sources. About five of the 26-member council initially backed the larger rate cut.

The pro-50-bp-rate-cut camp took the stance that the tariffs that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has been promising could add more downward pressure on the ECB's already reduced projection of 1.1% growth in 2025.

The group, though, quickly yielded to the smaller cut, as the policymakers had little desire to rush decisions amid an uncertain backdrop, Reuters said.

The euro strengthened ~0.2% against the U.S. dollar to $1.051. Invesco CurrencyShares ETF (NYSEARCA:FXE) was little changed in U.S. midday trading. iShares Core MSCI Europe ETF (NYSEARCA:IEUR) slipped 0.3% and iShares MSCI Europe Financials ETF (NASDAQ:EUFN) dipped 0.4%.

data.title: KeyBanc cuts rating on T-Mobile US, upgrades Charter Communications
data.description:
Analysts at KeyBanc made changes to their coverage of Communication Services stocks by downgrading their investment rating on T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS) and upgrading Charter Communications (NASDAQ:CHTR).

The research firm cut its rating on TMUS to "sector weight" from "overweight," based on valuation, which they think is expensive. They also think the stock is trading at a meaningful premium to peers and believe there are more attractive alternatives.

"We worry the market is moving toward a converged offering that T-Mobile is playing catch-up to address," KeyBanc said in a research note published on Wednesday.

They expect EBITDA growth to decelerate at TMUS due to investments in the business and headwinds from ACP and wholesale. They also see FCF growth to slow due to higher capital spending and cash taxes.

KeyBanc upgraded CHTR to "overweight" from "sector weight" and thinks the stock is attractively valued and can continue to work higher as broadband subscriber trends improve and cost efficiencies drive EBITDA growth in 2025, among other reasons.

The research firm noted CHTR’s rural build is accelerating and appreciated the company's cost cutting initiatives implemented so far. They see capital spending for the company peaking next year and moving meaningfully lower thereafter, which in turn should drive FCF higher.

TMUS does not have a PT from KeyBanc. CHTR was assigned a PT of $500, implying an upside of 32%.

TMUS is up nearly 45% and CHTR is down 1.5% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index is up 27.6%.

data.title: Long-term mortgage rates fall for the third straight week, affordability still an issue
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates were down for the third consecutive week, but affordability issues persisted, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.60% as of December 12, down from 6.69% last week and 6.95% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.84%, compared to 5.96% in the prior week and 6.38% a year ago.

"The combination of mortgage rate declines, firm consumer income growth and a bullish stock market have increased homebuyer demand in recent weeks," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"While the outlook for the housing market is improving, the improvement is limited given that homebuyers continue to face stiff affordability headwinds," Khater added.

data.title: Costco rejects 98% of Teamsters' demands
data.description:
Costco (NASDAQ:COST) is “treading dangerous waters” the head of the Teamsters union warned on Thursday after the retail giant rejected 98% of the union’s demands.

"The clock is ticking, and instead of engaging seriously at the bargaining table, Costco continues to waste time with surface bargaining," said Teamsters General President Sean M. O'Brien.

Earlier this month, the union filed unfair labor practice charges against Costco (COST) for expelling union representatives, harassing and intimidating workers for wearing Teamsters buttons and attire, and changing the locks on union bulletin boards to prevent future updates. The International Brotherhood of Teamsters represents 18,000 Costco (COST) employees across the country.

With the current contract – ratified in October 2022 -- set to expire in 7 weeks, the union is negotiating for paid family leave, bereavement policies, sick time, safeguards against surveillance, and establishing critical language in the contract on seniority. If the company does not present a collective bargaining agreement before Jan 31, Costco (COST) employees who are members of the union will go on strike.

Costco (COST) has not yet responded to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment.

data.title: Ciena soars following Q4 results as cloud, AI drive networking demand
data.description:
Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) shares soared following its fourth quarter fiscal 2024 results, despite coming up short of the consensus estimate on earnings per share.

Despite mixed results for the quarter ended November 2, the networking equipment and software provider gave a strong outlook due to cloud and artificial intelligence demand.

"As Cloud and AI drive bandwidth demand across the network, we are positioned for accelerated revenue growth and market share expansion moving forward," said Ciena CEO Gary Smith.

"CIEN expects FY25 and three-year growth to be in the 8% to 11% range, versus the prior target of 6% to 8%, and growth is reflective of cyclical recovery within telco capex as order trends are starting to improve, and CIEN is seeing order growth exceed revenue growth," said Evercore analyst Amit Daryanani, in a Thursday note.

Evercore increased its price target on the stock to $85 from $65.

"The big upside lever though is around a stronger cloud spending environment in FY25 and beyond as CIEN starts to see tailwinds from AI investments by cloud companies extend beyond compute to other parts of the DC," Daryanani added.

Ciena also announced it had repurchased 2.1M shares for $132M during the fourth quarter.

While Ciena was up 13% by noon trading on Thursday, other networking stocks were relatively flat, including Cisco Systems (CSCO) +1%, Juniper Networks (JNPR) +0.1% and Arista Networks (ANET) -0.4%.
